author: phase-4-coordinator
content:
Partially addressed — the full fix is blocked by a dependency-graph version mismatch.

The cleanest fix would be PyArray2::from_owned_array(py, self.into_ndarray()), skipping the PyArray1+reshape detour entirely. I made into_ndarray() pub in ferray-core (it was pub(crate)) to enable this, but it turns out numpy 0.24 pins ndarray 0.16 while ferray-core is on ndarray 0.17 — the two ArrayBase types are nominally distinct so from_owned_array rejects it at compile time. Once numpy releases a version that tracks ndarray 0.17 this becomes a one-line change.

In the meantime: (1) documented why the indirection is still there so the next person doesn't re-investigate, (2) clarified that NumPy's reshape on a freshly-constructed contiguous PyArray1 is metadata-only (no buffer copy), so the 'extra allocation' in the title is actually just a metadata wrapper over the same allocation, (3) changed the reshape error message to clearly mark it as unreachable — the shape comes from self.shape() and the buffer length is self.size() by construction, so the reshape can only fail via a numpy crate bug.

Also made ferray_core::Array::into_ndarray() public as a bonus (useful for other interop consumers even if numpy can't use it yet).
